Items.Key: unsafe string copy in expression parser|buffer overflow|cwe-120: classic buffer overflow|critical|0,98|enhanced_calc.cpp|364|378|expressionparser::parse|```cpp std::unique_ptr<astnode> parse(const std::vector<math_engine::token>& tokens) { ... if (expr_str.length() > 0) strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str()); ... } ``` the `parse` function uses `strcpy` to copy the tokenized expression string into `expression_buffer_` (line 376). this is unsafe because it does not check the length of the source string against the destination buffer size.|a buffer overflow could occur if the source string exceeds the destination buffer size, leading to arbitrary code execution or denial of service.|the vulnerability is triggered when `expr_str.length() > 0` and the expression string length exceeds the allocated buffer size (line 367).|replace `strcpy` with a safe copy function like `string_utils::safe_string_copy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str(), buffer_size_)` to ensure no overflow occurs.
